problem: Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease?
mechanism: mRNA therapy to upregulate/downregulate gene expression
hypothesis: The key to Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease? is mRNA therapy to upregulate/downregulate gene expression — specifically mRNA therapy to — which would help 50,000,000 people affected.

experiment.protocol: Retrospective cohort analysis with 15 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way. — Marcus Aurelius
